title: Why was Gendo blamed for losing the spear
display_title: Why was Gendo blamed for losing the spear
wikitext:
[]: Why was Gendo blamed for losing the spear?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Wed Jul 24, 2024 11:12 am Hello there! As we all know the Angel Arael was defeated with a spear throw, and as a result the spear of Longius was lost. Gendo tells SEELE that he had to use the spear to kill the angel but SEELE doesnt believe Gendo and suspect his future betrayal. During the Angel attack when Gendo decides to use the Spear of Longius , Fuyutuski also says that he uses the Angel attack as pretext to dispose of the spear. And indeed Gendo has ulterior motives and wants to dispose of the spear but I see one question unresolved: Why do SEELE and Fuyutuski think Gendo wanted to dispose of the spear? Both SEELE and Fuyutuski could see the fight against Arael unfold and that NERV was getting pushed in. I don't see how NERV could have defeated the Angel so why are SEELE and Fuyutuski so confident in their suspicion of Gendo ? What could he have done differently?

[]: Re: Why was Gendo blamed for losing the spear?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Jul 25, 2024 4:18 am Seele has always left the strategic destruction of Angels in the hands of Gendo, Misato and the Nerv crew and they certainly have very high expectations that they devise a strategy within seconds of an Angel appearing and then destroy it with minimal damage and costs (a bit far-fetched if you ask me). It seems that using the spear is the last possible resort that would only be used in endgame scenarios, which could be seen as debatable as only Asuka was the one being compromised. She refuses to retreat due to her fear of losing and we are left in a bit of a stalemate situation. We know Gendo doesn't give a toss about eva pilots so it would seem suspicious why he would save Asuka using the spear as the weapon. Obviously, it was good that the spear was used in the end, but I think Seele was expecting them to initially defeat the angel using other tactics. There was a brief debate about sending eva into orbit and deploying unit 1 and avoiding getting hit but were rejected. Alternatively, they could have found some way of retrieving Unit 2 and then brainstorming another approach after all the Angel poses no threat in space and cannot get to Lilith.

[]: Re: Why was Gendo blamed for losing the spear?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jul 25, 2024 11:11 am Gendo got rid of the spear because Seele needed it for their version of Third Impact. Gendo didn't need it because he had Rei, Lilith, and the Adam embryo. Gendo didn't expect the spear to come flying back.
